2604.13979.title: Leveraging LLM-GNN Integration for Open-World Question Answering over Knowledge Graphs
2604.13979.paper_summary: Open-world Question Answering (OW-QA) over knowledge graphs (KGs) aims to answer questions over incomplete or evolving KGs. Traditional KGQA assumes a closed world where answers must exist in the KG, limiting real-world applicability. In contrast, open-world QA requires inferring missing knowledge based on graph structure and context. Large language models (LLMs) excel at language understanding but lack structured reasoning. Graph neural networks (GNNs) model graph topology but struggle with semantic interpretation. Existing systems integrate LLMs with GNNs or graph retrievers. Some support open-world QA but rely on structural embeddings without semantic grounding. Most assume observed paths or complete graphs, making them unreliable under missing links or multi-hop reasoning. We present GLOW, a hybrid system that combines a pre-trained GNN and an LLM for open-world KGQA. The GNN predicts top-k candidate answers from the graph structure. These, along with relevant KG facts, are serialized into a structured prompt (e.g., triples and candidates) to guide the LLM's reasoning. This enables joint reasoning over symbolic and semantic signals, without relying on retrieval or fine-tuning. To evaluate generalization, we introduce GLOW-BENCH, a 1,000-question benchmark over incomplete KGs across diverse domains. GLOW outperforms existing LLM-GNN systems on standard benchmarks and GLOW-BENCH, achieving up to 53.3% and an average 38% improvement. GitHub code and data are available.
2604.13979.paper_summary_zh: 開放世界問題回答（OW-QA）在知識圖譜（KGs）上旨在回答不完整或不斷演變的KGs上的問題。傳統的KGQA假設一個封閉的世界，答案必須存在於KG中，這限制了其在現實世界中的應用。相對而言，開放世界QA需要根據圖結構和上下文推斷缺失的知識。大型語言模型（LLMs）在語言理解方面表現優異，但缺乏結構化推理。圖神經網絡（GNNs）建模圖拓撲，但在語義解釋上存在困難。現有系統將LLMs與GNNs或圖檢索器整合在一起。有些支持開放世界QA，但依賴於結構嵌入而沒有語義基礎。大多數假設觀察到的路徑或完整圖，這使得它們在缺失連結或多跳推理的情況下不可靠。我們提出GLOW，一個混合系統，結合了預訓練的GNN和LLM以進行開放世界KGQA。GNN根據圖結構預測前k個候選答案。這些候選答案以及相關的KG事實被序列化成結構化提示（例如，三元組和候選者）以引導LLM的推理。這使得在符號和語義信號上進行聯合推理，而不依賴於檢索或微調。為了評估泛化能力，我們引入GLOW-BENCH，一個涵蓋多個領域的1,000題不完整KG的基準測試。GLOW在標準基準和GLOW-BENCH上超越現有的LLM-GNN系統，達到最高53.3%和平均38%的改進。GitHub代碼和數據可用。
2604.13979.author: Hussein Abdallah et.al.
2604.13979.authors: Hussein Abdallah, Ibrahim Abdelaziz, Panos Kalnis, Essam Mansour
